title: Pipeline Observability, Incident Response, Governance, and Improvement · Automation Case: Ci Run Log Artifact Retention Cost
summary: Delivery slows while storage cost grows, teams rerun failures without diagnosis, logs expose identifiers, exceptions never expire, and no one can reconstruct which change reached which users. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci run log artifact retention cost increment in delivery operations and governance defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set log, artifact, cache, attestation, test-report, and deployment-evidence retention from recovery, audit, privacy, quota, and cost needs.
Instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Set log, artifact, cache, attestation, test-report, and deployment-evidence retention from recovery, audit, privacy, quota, and cost needs. A defensible solution applies Ci Run Log Artifact Retention Cost, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to delivery operations and governance defense.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-ci-observability-incidents-governance-5
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-observability-incidents-governance-5
    artifact: delivery-operations.yaml
    decision: worked ci observability incidents governance 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      metrics: queue-critical-path-failure-class-recovery
      cardinality: bounded
      privacy: actor-and-input-minimized
      retention: risk-cost-policy
      incident: contain-preserve-restore-notify
      first-causal-failure: reconstructed
      credential-response: revoke-and-rotate
      artifact-recovery: prior-verified-digest
      exception: owner-reason-expiry
      drill: quarterly
      improvement: outcome-reviewed
    verify: reconstruct one delivery incident from event workflow runner dependency artifact deployment telemetry audit containment and recovery evidence
    failure: Reject unbounded metric labels, forever retention, blind reruns, workflow cancellation as containment, or non-expiring central policy exceptions.
    repair: Measure useful flow safely, retain by need, diagnose first cause, contain authority and mutations, restore verified artifacts, expire exceptions, and drill.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
